zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux於1329年出生在法國皮卡迪索姆波福斯時，她的父親Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy25歲，母親Marie de Vianden35歲。她於大約1363年與Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux于1329年出生在法国皮卡迪索姆波福斯时，她的父亲Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy25岁，母亲Marie de Vianden35岁。她于大约1363年与Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Coucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon): nickname from Middle English couch(e) ‘hump, hunch’, used for a hunchback. In Cornwall and Devon it is usually pronounced as Cooch.
English (Cornwall and Devon): variant of   Gooch  .

English (Cornwall and Devon): from Middle English coche, couche ‘bed, couch, tablecloth’ (Old French couche, culche). It could have been a metonymic occupational name for someone who made such items or a nickname for a lazy individual. Alternatively, it could have been applied to someone who produced couchwork, a luxurious embroidery in which gold or silver thread and jewels were sewn into satin or silk robes.

variants.name: Gooch
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the rare Middle English personal name Goche (also found as Joche). It was current in East Anglia from the early 12th to the early 13th centuries as a variant of Anglo-Norman French Go(s)ce, Jo(s)ce, a pet form of Old French Goscelin.
English: alternatively, a nickname from Anglo-Norman French gouge (from Latin gobio), the nominative form of Old French goujon (from Latin gobionem) ‘gudgeon’, perhaps for a gullible person.

Welsh: in southwestern England, possibly an Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’, though the sound change is irregular. Compare Gough . It may also be a variant of Cornish and Welsh Couch , with the same meaning.

variants.name: Gush
meanings: English (Devon and Dorset): probably a variant of Gudge, itself a variant of Couch or Gooch .

variants.name: Coach
meanings: Possibly also an Americanized form of French Canadian or French   Coache  .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux was born in 1329, in Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, her father, Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy, was 25 and her mother, Marie de Vianden, was 35. She married Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean about 1363. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux est née en 1329, à Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, son père, Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Marie de Vianden, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean environ 1363. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux 1329 in Boves, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy 25 und ihre Mutter Marie de Vianden 35. Sie hat um 1363 Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux è nata il nel 1329 nel Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, sua padre, Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Marie de Vianden, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean circa 1363. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meauxが1329年にフランスピカーディソンメボベスで生まれた時，父Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucyは25歳，母Marie de Viandenは35歳であった。約1363年にGaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porceanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はスコットランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux(이)가 1329년 프랑스 피카디 솜 보브스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie de Vianden(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1363년 Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 스컷랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux nasceu em 1329, em Boves, Somme, Picardie, França, seu pai, Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Marie de Vianden, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean aproximadamente 1363. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux родилась в 1329 г. в местности бовесь, сомм, пикарди, Франция, её отцу по имени Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie de Vianden, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean около 1363 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux nació en 1329, en Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Enguerrand VI de Guînes-Coucy, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Marie de Vianden, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Gaucher de Chatillon III  cte de Porcean aproximadamente 1363. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en Scotland.
